risk_if_not_implemented: Without Periodic Review, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Secure Baseline Configurations
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to develop, document and maintain secure baseline configurations for Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) that are consistent with industry-accepted system hardening standards.
compensating_control_1.justification: Secure Baseline Configurations (CFG-02) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Periodic Review (CFG-03.1) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Prohibit Installation Without Privileged Status
compensating_control_2.description: Automated mechanisms exist to prohibit software installations without explicitly assigned privileged status.
compensating_control_2.justification: Prohibit Installation Without Privileged Status (END-03) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Periodic Review (CFG-03.1) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
